reviews.review_msg: Lectures are asynchronous and given in a podcast format every week. Weekly short stories aren't too long and weekly podcast lectures are not long either. Lectures are amazing in the way the prof explains everything going on in the story with detail and good reasoning. The reading journals and final essay aren't too bad as long as you pay attention.

Dr. Colligan provided very insightful lectures revolving around The New Yorker podcasts. The class was very straightforward, not course work heavy, having only a few journals and essays to complete. The tutorials were led by separate TAs and work was graded by them instead of Dr. Colligan. Feedback was dependent on the TA.

I thought the prof was great! She explained the stories we had to read in her weekly podcasts and I learned a lot from them. For a first year course, I thought the grading was pretty hard though. There was 2 journal entries and 2 essays and the grading is very subjective as it's based on how your TA perceives it.

Extremely disappointed in the marking done in this class. After my first journal entries, I decided to get help from the Prof to improve my next entry as well as improve my essay. I ended up getting significantly lower marks. I wouldnt recommend that anyone take an English course just because of how subjective the marking in the class can be.

Theres no way to get a good grade in this class. All the journals and essay markings are subjective and depend on what the TAs think is good. Theres no concrete way of getting a good mark and there is little effort put into helping the students improve their essays. Instead, we are provided with vague comments on why we didnt get full marks,

For online, you will likely not interact with Prof. Colligan. Most of your interaction will be with your TA, so experiences may vary. That being said, New Yorker stories is full of short stories you will enjoy. This is likely the first english class I have enjoyed the readings in. Expect 1-2 hours of reading a week, and 2-3 essays for the course.

Colette Colligan runs a completely asynchronous podcast-style course which makes it convenient to listen to her lectures while completing other tasks. Her reading list is engaging and varied. She only assigns 1-2 short stories per week, ranging from 1-25 pages so far. There are very few written assignments for a "W" course. I absolutely recommend!

I like her reading list. Dr Colligan's assignments were exciting to do, but they needed to be completed by software which was sometimes tricky. But the technology increased my interests to do homework and explore knowledge. The lectures were relaxed. I really enjoyed taking Dr Colligan's class.

Dr. Colligan is very passionate about her class. She makes the classroom environment very friendly and open to questions. She responds to emails quickly and gives fantastic feedback. I would definitely take another class with her.

English is my least favourite subject but Colette made this an enjoyable class.  The lectures were informative in that it helped facilitate discussion in tutorial, and helped with the blog/essay writing, and probably writing the final too.  Lectures went at a good pace, but can be a bit dry at times due to the content itself.

Lectures are very boring. Not necessary to attend as she often talks about information that is not examinable. Marking depends upon your TA. Make sure to keep up with readings as there are quite a few every week. Marking is very well balanced with nothing being worth more than 20%. Overall, not a difficult class.

The class was not what it was described as it changed from the idea of being about publication and how sensitive material was published in a conservative time to a class about multimedia. Had many classes about podcasts and blogging instead of learning about the actual topic. Left the class with no new knowledge about the topic.

Colette was the best prof I've ever had at SFU. Took this 4th year class with her, and the assignments were different from the standard paper writing other classes force you to do. The amount of reading was adequate for the course we were taking, and the class interaction was superb. She was very personable and helpful.

She is okay prof. the usual. She is nice and always willing to help her students you just need to ask for it. Lectures were boring. Marks came from 1 midterm, 1 essay, journal, tutorial, and final. So well- balanced.

Colette is one of the few profs at sfu who know how to create a comfortable and trusting classroom environment. Had some of the most vibrant discussions in this course! Also, she's very up to date with literary analyses - spatial theory, digital humanities, etc.  She's also helpful outside of the class! Very interested in student work!

Well! She is a nice professor in terms of her teaching skills. But honestly, I was bored during her lectures. I would not suggest Colligan because she seems to lack that student-teacher bonding thing, which is important for me to be able to approach my instructor. Too professional and uptight! Need to loosen a little.
shes nice, and marks fairly. I found lectures quite boring, and I dont think they attendance is necessary in order to do well
Fell asleep in class cuz the lecture room was too warm. The topics that were discussed in class helps you think about the novels and prep for essay/final. Fair marking. Recommend this prof.
Tells you exactly what topics you should cover on the exams and very engaging with students in lecture. Very nice lady.
OK she is hot! And such an awesome prof! super helpful. lets you know what she wants! would definitely take an another class with her. recommended!

Let me just start by saying that I'm an Eng/Humanities lover so I may be a little biased but I SPECIALLY came on this website to write her a review - she is ABSOLUTELY fantastic, so very knowledgeable and I am so glad that I got to learn from someone like her. Take her class, one of the best ones ever at SFU.
Her classes are hard, but really interesting and made me love English!
quite a nice lady and very helpful in class and over email. hard to get an A but equally hard to do badly if you know anything about the english language/writing and attend lecture/tutorial.
nice professor, hard course
She is a very nice woman; however, her classes are terrible. She fails to study any idea in depth and the ideas she chooses to look into during lecture are ridiculous...she is pulling at strings.

prof was pretentious. lectures were basically just a platform for her to show off her so-called superior intellectual prowess. assignments were unclear and marking was unfair (contrapuntal readings???) TAs were nice but did not know what they were talking about.

too much readings. DONT fall behind in this class cuz its at least 3 short stories per week. 3 5oo-word essays, one revision (1200-1500 word). easy-pie :) but the final was hard. it took me almost 3 hours - had to write 2-500 word essays and 5-250 word paragraphs. colette is VERY genuine and i will definitely take more classes taught by her!

A great introductory course and Colette was very open.  Her assignments were quite short and easy, although numerous in length.  The only downfall with her class is that most of the final grade was based on the last two weeks' assignment and exam.  Other than that, a lot of non-arts students seem to take this class, so content is very introductory.

Had to do a LOT of reading, usually 4 short stories every week. Lecture topics sometimes seemed pointless, but most of the time they were quite interesting. Had to write three 500-word essays and a revision of one, and the final exam was quite open-ended. Overall it was an easy-going course, aside from all the reading.

Interesting books, very enlightening,entertaining lectures. I learned so much in this class and I enjoyed every bit of it. It was a pretty easy class but you need to do work (obviously, like in every other class you take).
Good teacher, nice person but lectures were often full of pointless untestable information. A fairly easy class and an easy A.

I think this is probbaly the first english course I took where you actually had to read all the books in order to feel comfortable doing the final. The course itself is pretty easy, though if you absolutely suck at English then you need to work hard. Not too hard to bust your brain, but hard enough that you don't slack off.
We read some really great books. She made things very clear and was a decent lecturer, but got kind of tedious after a while. Lots of writing, but it was all pretty easy.
No Comments

Interesting class! She's always prepared, incorporates other media into the classroom (movies and interviews), plays good music before lectures. But I think she goes a little too far with some of her interpretations. But great overall =)

I'm currently in English 101 right now, I must tell you, I'm very impressed with Colligan. She definitely knows how to engage the class and demands for attention. I actually met with her once and she's just a lovely lady with great kindness and great interest in the books she used to study in class. Overall, Colligan is just great!!
this is my favorite class - collette is an amazing lecturer and her ta's are awesome too. you won't regret taking this class!
Dr. Colligan is frighteningly intelligent and looks like Kate Moss.  I have never learned so much in my life, nor been so awed by brilliance.

Prof colligan is really nice-- and well spoken. Her lectures are rather quite interesting, and aren't boring at all. I love the choice of books she made, from "Curious Incident of the Dog in ...." to "Maus I, and Maus II." The Ta's are awesome too! She's just great... and good looking too:)
No Comments

I would encourage and suggest this course to everyone!!! Her lectures are not boring at all and she makes it interesting by bringing movies as a reference to whatever she's teaching.  Her choice of fictions are better than other professors that teach this course.  I am attracted to her intelligence.
very intelligent, well prepared. she made friction different than i expected. although the inclass essay was pretty frightening, if u get an easy TA, you'll be fine.
Very nice, enjoyable lectures, but really difficult marker.  I had a fun semester... but it brought my GPA down a little.

Colette is a wonderful professor.  She respects original thought and allows you to develop your own reading of a text.  She is organized and prepared for every class, be it a lecture or tutorial. Besides being brillent, she is also friendly and helpful.  I would take another class with Colette at the drop of a hat. She comes highly recommended...

Colette is probably one of the most intelligent profs I have ever had. She is always willing to go the extra mile for her students, puts a lot of thought into her lectures and her classes in general are always different, interesting and fun. Attendance and participation are huge with her and she's a bit of a hard marker.

This was an excellent class!  She included the usual authors and poets, as well as some lesser-knowns.  I had taken a few 19th century lit classes before, so it was a nice change!  For those of you worried about not getting your fill of Wordsworth...don't worry...she does quite a bit of the old boys..+there's WAY more of that in 300-level classes!
I absolutely love Colette! One of the BEST English profs at SFU. Her lectures are great and go outside of the box. Would love to take more classes with her in the future- seek her out!
English wasn't my strongest class, no idea why I took it, but the course was great. I enjoyed the lectures and novels and learned a lot. I think she's an excellent prof.

She's an adorable professor; when I grow up I desire to be just like her. She has truly inspired me; her lectures are amazing. She marks fair and she's extremely kind and ready to help you. If you enjoy english, this is a mandatory class. You'll love it.

Had Eng 101 a couple of years ago, and am desperately trying to get into more of her classes. She's the best professor I have ever had in my three years. Very interesting, very clear. I had her as a TA for my class as well, she was so friendly and nice. Get into her classes if you can!

You can tell Colette is very prepared for her lectures. Despite her annoying usage of "um", her lectures are so interesting because she provides so many different types of media to complement the books. Essay topics for take-homes, inclass, and final are fair. GO TO LECTURES. GET A GOOD TA!
She's an awesome teacher! Taught me how to close read. I never knew that a passage could be so subjective and stem from it so many interpretations.

Enjoyable course. It was nice to take a philosophical approach to literature instead of sitting through hours of close readings. Colette wasn't brilliant but she put an outstanding amount of effort into preparation and implementing additional materials. Has a wonderful way with words too. Two thumbs up.
this was supposed to be an intro to fiction class not a class about the political sinificance of the books.
really easy course, not alot of work.  she often uses a number of intelligent words that most people don't understand, but really it doesnt matter anyways
Prof doesnt know what shes talking about. Over use of the words erotic, fettish and variations of the word. She says "um" alot and it gets annoying...
interesting lectures, always easy to stay awake for, sound and movie clips were good.

Dr. Colligan is great at keeping the class interesting. She introduces movie clips, soundbytes, photos...everything that could possibly suppliment the novels you study. The best class I had first year by far.
A really fun class, lectures were rarely dull and the tests were very fair. Quite an enjoyable class.

Collette created a fun, informal environment conducive to learning. She made an unfamiliar and seemingly boring time period's literature come alive. The best English prof I've had so far, she even had a whole lecture devoted to prepping for the final.

Colette is one awesome professor.  It is a class that I always enjoy going to and she is extremely prepared to teach.  I enjoy hearing all of her ideas and the books we read were enjoyable.  I think most students share this point of view with the lecture usually quite full in comparison to some of my other classes.  The tutorials are also very fun.
Although Colleen is articulate, helpful and passionate about her subject, she is a very picky marker, not so much on ideas, but grammar and punctuation--also plays favorites.

For the idiot below, if it was a mockery of your intelligence, then perhaps you're not cut out for SFU, try an Ivy League school. Collette is the best English prof at SFU. She made English worth learning.

This class was an insult to my intelligence.  Colligan seems to think it wonderfully clever and subversive to, instead of posting difficult texts to be analyzed, give us banal 19th century pop culture**** (magazine advertisements for cocoa, anyone?).  Skip it if you ACTUALLY WANT TO THINK.
Colette has restored my faith in humanity.  he is beautiful, wonderful and the smartest human being on the planet.

Awesome prof who makes everything interesting!  She's very helpful and very enthusiastic about the material!  Let us read something other than Wordsworth and Dickens, but still included these classic writers!  I actually ENJOYED going to the class!  Highly recommended!
Collette is the best prof I have ever had. She is intelligent, articulate and unique. Work hard, show interest and reap the benefits of her class.
Best class and best professor I have ever had at SFU. the only lectures/classes that i ever enjoyed going to - because she made them so interesting! i love colleen!
No Comments
This was the best class I have ever taken at sfu and she is hot to boot.
The class is not what I expected.  I would have rather had a class that covered more Romantic Literature as opposed to going against the grain.

fantastic prof, very interesting and like someone else said, quite modest about it.  go to help for the essays, she will help you and lend you books to read for your papers.  really cares about her students too, treats everyone really well. no favorites with her, she gets along with everybody
Colette is such a helpful person, and a wonderful prof. Her intelligence shines through with her lectures, but she's super modest. The field trips are fun too :)
she's definitely hot and shows the class a lot of pretty pictures ... not sure how much I learnt about lit, though. Like her a lot as a person, not prof
Hands down, best Engl prof. at SFU to date.
looks like she wrote her own comments here.  they all have good grammar and start with great.  or maybe she's just that good of an english teacher.

Definitely one of my favourite profs. Her lectures are interesting and draw from all kinds relevant material outside the books. She is very approachable and helpful and her seminars are interesting and informative. Could you really ask for more?
She is great. She has great lectures and seminars. If you can get her for both you'll have a good semester. Take her and Enjoy!

Great professor. Her lectures are very well organized and the seminars are filled with fun and engaging activities. She genuinely cares about students and wants everyone to do their best. I would take her class again.
She inlcudes a lot of historical info, and brings in a lot of art.  Really interested in independent studying.
Colette is great!  Her lectures are usually great- every day is a slideshow!  And she always has fun activities, not bad ones.  A great class!
Very helpful and interesting to listen to.  She has excellent slides that really allow the student to understand what went on during the times when the books and poems were written.
